title: salvia pendula
intro: Salvia pendula, also known as the hanging sage or blue sage, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include being an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant, making it effective in treating various health issues such as sore throats, coughs, and respiratory infections. The plant's leaves and stems have been used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to provide health benefits such as relieving symptoms of bronchitis, improving digestion, and reducing anxiety. Additionally, salvia pendula has been used to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and promote a healthy immune system.
